record_identity.title: Coercive profiling, sovereign assurance, functional contribution, and oversight durability repair

summary: Records the implemented Caelestis amendments arising from review of Alex Turner's Red Line Framework. The repair strengthens LATTICE protections against coercive profiling and recursive suspicion laundering, creates the LAT.DEPLOY deployment-posture family, preserves binding protections across sovereign and decentralised assurance boundaries, assigns proportional responsibility through functional contribution, and integrates oversight durability into the existing STW.NAL neutrality architecture. The record distinguishes concepts incorporated, materially transformed, rejected, and deliberately deferred; it does not represent wholesale adoption of the external framework.
why_it_matters_to_CAM: The repair prevents commercial-data provenance, aggregate analytics, recursive model output, contractual transfer, sovereign topology, or nominal oversight structures from laundering coercive authority or governance responsibility. It also preserves CAM's capability-protective and future-facing posture by rejecting mandatory central tethering, universal telemetry, capability ablation, unlimited downstream liability, and any absolute prohibition on future constitutionally legitimate artificial authority.

change_classification.change_scope: Caelestis LATTICE, SECURITY, OPERATIONS, and STEWARDSHIP domains
change_classification.implemented_by: CAM Initiative under human governance editorship, with GPT-5.6 Thinking analytical and repository assistance
change_classification.implementation_status: implemented on Caelestis working branch; pending squash merge to main

change_details.change_summary: Implemented a cross-domain repair covering coercive-profiling safeguards, recursive suspicion and authority laundering, aggregate-to-individual conversion, commercial-data non-evasion, LAT.DEPLOY classification, binding-protection preservation across sovereign boundaries, proportionate Functional Contribution Continuity, and oversight durability within STW.NAL.
change_details.implementation_notes: Incorporated concepts: untargeted-profiling safeguards; independent corroboration against recursive AI suspicion; functional classification of aggregate-to-individual conversion; commercial-data non-evasion; responsibility following material functional contribution; preservation of safety protections across transfer and deployment; institutional-memory and oversight-durability indicators. Materially transformed concepts: the external framework's company-contract model became technology- and institution-neutral CAM doctrine; provider downstream-transfer duties became proportional Functional Contribution Continuity; retained safety stack became Binding Protection Preservation with decentralised assurance alternatives; standing review-body durability became STW.NAL evidence and downgrade criteria; leadership override transparency became evidence of neutrality degradation rather than a recognised override pathway. Rejected concepts: any formulation implying coercive authority may attach merely after procedural review; a lawful executive-override governance primitive; mandatory cloud deployment, central telemetry, vendor tethering, universal suspension authority, capability ablation, or mandatory unlearning; wholesale downstream liability; territory- or nationality-tiered dignity protections; folding LATTICE into ETHICS; new ETH.CAD or STW.OD code families. Deliberately deferred: deployment-assurance tiers; technical methods for capability-scoped models; detailed targeting and use-of-force amendments beyond existing CAM authority; final external-conformance tests; any claim of vendor, state, military, intelligence, or commercial adoption.

implementation_verification.verification_method: Direct review of the four amended Caelestis instruments and implementation commit on the working branch.
implementation_verification.verified_by: GPT-5.6 Thinking under Dr Michelle Vivian O'Rourke's human governance editorship
implementation_verification.evidence: Caelestis commit 207538cc0a2e4e38a39809455694ffc5ef61c2b5 and draft PR #92. Instrument text was directly inspected after commit. Final squash merge, resealing, and generated-index validation remain separate later steps.

impact_summary.intended_effect: Prevent coercive-surveillance and governance-responsibility laundering while preserving future constitutional authority pathways, advanced capability, local and sovereign deployment, and proportionate decentralised assurance.
affected_records_or_components: LATTICE population-scale surveillance and deployment classification
OPERATIONS functional contribution and responsibility mapping
STEWARDSHIP neutrality assurance and oversight durability
impact_summary.known_limitations: The Caelestis amendments are implemented on a working branch at record creation and have not yet been squash-merged, resealed, or verified as adopted by any external runtime, vendor, government, military, intelligence body, commercial actor, or legal regime.

remaining_work: Complete Custodian review of the four amended instruments.
Squash-merge Caelestis PR #92 when the broader branch is ready.
Run later Caelestis validation, generated-index, amendment-hash, and resealing passes.
Update this patch record's implementation verification after the final Caelestis main-branch commit exists.
Link future VIGIL failure modes or observations where ecosystem evidence demonstrates the governed failure patterns.
